授業目的: This course explores how culture influences the human mind and behavior. Students will be introduced to the field of cross-cultural psychology with a focus on how individuals feel, think, and behave as a function of their cultural context. The course emphasizes differences between Western (primarily European American) and East Asian (primarily Japanese) psychological frameworks.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（心理Ｂ）（CIEC）/Japanese Psychology B（国際学部）
授業方法: The course consists of 15 sessions: 12 face-to-face classes, 1 on-demand startup session, and 2 on-demand reading sessions. The on-demand reading sessions are assigned between face-to-face meetings.

第1回:
【Startup Week】
"Online class（On-demand format)" ／ 《90minutes》

Please go through the course syllabus and get yourself familiar with it. If you have decided to take this course please submit the following assignment in word format before the start of formal classes.

Please introduce yourself 
who you are
  • your background such as your country, university, major and what year are you now
  • anything unique about yourself that you would like to share such as your hobbies our things you are good at
  • your course expectations

Also please answer following two questions: There is no right or wrong answer so please feel free to write what comes to your mind.
  • What is culture to you? 
  • What comes to your mind when you think about "Japanese culture"?

Link to submit the word file: Self-Introduction
Please change the file name to "student number-name" before uploading the file.
第2回:
【Core Weeks①】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Introduction to cross-cultural psychology (Defining culture, WEIRD psychology, Types of universality)
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Self and Personality 1 (Independent vs Interdependent self-construals)
第4回:
【Core Weeks③】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Self and Personality 2 (Face and Dignity cultures)
第5回:
【Core Weeks④】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Self and Personality 3 (Cognitive dissonance and free choice paradigm across cultures)
第6回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（On-demand format)" ／ 《90minutes》
Post reading: Self and Personality

Please Read: Kim, Y. H., Cohen, D., & Au, W. T. (2010). The jury and abjury of my peers: The self in face and dignity cultures. JPSP, 98(6), 904. Submit comprehension response.

Few students will be randomly selected and asked short conceptual questions in the following class.
第7回:
【Core Weeks⑤】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Motivation 1 (Self-esteem, better than average effect and unrealistic optimism across cultures)
第8回:
【Core Weeks⑥】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Motivation 2 (Self-enhancement vs self-improvement)
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑦】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Motivation 3 (Agency and control, choices across cultures, fit in vs stick-out)
第10回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（On-demand format)" ／ 《90minutes》
Post reading: Motivation

Please Read: Heine, S. J., Lehman, D. R., Markus, H. R., & Kitayama, S. (1999). Is there a universal need for positive self-regard? Psychological Review, 106(4), 766. Submit comprehension response.

Few students will be randomly selected and asked short conceptual questions in the following class.
第11回:
【Core Weeks⑧】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Communication (Direct vs Indirect, self-censorship across cultures)
Groups assignment for the project presentations
第12回:
【Core Weeks⑨】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Socialization (Cultural acquisition, socialization processes)
第13回:
【Core Weeks⑩】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Topic: Research methodology (tailored to cross-cultural psychology: main pitfalls to avoid)
第14回:
【Core Weeks⑪】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Project Presentations 1 (Group research presentations, Q&A and discussion)
第15回:
【Core Weeks⑫】
"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Project Presentations 2 (Group research presentations, Q&A and discussion)

教科書/Required texts2: Kim, Y. H., Cohen, D., & Au, W. T. (2010). The jury and abjury of my peers: The self in face and dignity cultures. JPSP, 98(6), 904.
教科書/Required texts3: Heine, S. J., Lehman, D. R., Markus, H. R., & Kitayama, S. (1999). Is there a universal need for positive self-regard? Psychological Review, 106(4), 766
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 授業外学修課題／Assignment, homework required outside class (06)
成績評価Grading3: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
成績評価Grading4: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)
成績評価Grading5: 備考　Note Students will form groups of 3–4 to conduct a research project on a cultural psychology topic of their choice (subject to instructor approval). The project involves reviewing relevant literature, designing a study framework, and presenting expected findings to the class during Sessions 14–15.Following the group presentation, each student will individually write an extension paper connecting the group’s research topic to a different concept covered in the course. For example, if a group presents on face vs. dignity cultures, one student might connect it to motivation, another to development, and so on. name: 【300】総合日本学習科目（心理Ｂ）／JSC Japanese Psychology B
担当者: アリ　サラマド（ALI SARMAD）
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
Students will form groups of 3–4 to conduct a research project on a cultural psychology topic of their choice (subject to instructor approval). The project involves reviewing relevant literature, designing a study framework, and presenting expected findings to the class during Sessions 14–15.
Following the group presentation, each student will individually write an extension paper connecting the group’s research topic to a different concept covered in the course. For example, if a group presents on face vs. dignity cultures, one student might connect it to motivation, another to development, and so on. This ensures each student demonstrates individual critical thinking beyond the group work.
